note: Gottweis et al., 'Accelerating scientific discovery with Co-Scientist', Nature 655:487-496 (2026), DOI 10.1038/s41586-026-10644-y. Gemini-based multi-agent system: Supervisor + 6 specialized agents (Generation, Reflection, Ranking, Evolution, Proximity, Meta-review) running an Elo-based tournament 'generate-debate-evolve' self-improving loop on an asynchronous task queue. Three end-to-end biomedical validations: (1) AML drug-repurposing with in vitro wet-lab cell-line assays; (2) liver-fibrosis novel-target discovery in human hepatic organoids (in vitro); (3) AMR cf-PICI mechanism as an in-silico recapitulation matched to an independent co-timed experimental study (refs 2, 21) -- not a system-run wet-lab. So system-run experimental wet-lab validation exists for 2 of 3 tasks.

C062.evidence: Both sub-claims confirmed by the paper. (a) 'multi-agent hypothesis-generation system': abstract explicitly introduces 'Co-Scientist, a multi-agent artificial intelligence (AI) system built on Gemini for structured scientific thinking and hypothesis generation' (p.487); Methods detail a Supervisor agent orchestrating 6 specialized worker agents in an asynchronous task-execution framework with an Elo tournament (init Elo=1,200) and a generate-debate-evolve loop (Fig.1b; Methods lines 1791-1918). (b) 'with experimental biomedical validation': 'we perform end-to-end wet-laboratory validations (laboratory experiments) of Co-Scientist-generated hypotheses' (lines 511-516); AML drug-repurposing validated by in vitro cell-line viability assays (Figs 3-4; e.g., binimetinib IC50 as low as 2 nM, novel candidate KIRA6 with 18-fold selectivity) and liver fibrosis validated in human hepatic organoids (lines 1564-1575). Manuscript wording is a fair, accurate summary.
C062.nuance: Optional precision only: system-run experimental (wet-lab) validation applies to 2 of the 3 showcased tasks. The third task (AMR / cf-PICI) is an in-silico recapitulation whose confirmation came from an independent co-timed experimental study, not from experiments driven by Co-Scientist. The manuscript's phrase 'with experimental biomedical validation' remains true regardless, so no change is required.

reverse_findings.fact: The AMR / cf-PICI task is presented as an in-silico recapitulation: Co-Scientist independently proposed the top hypothesis (cf-PICIs interact with diverse phage tails to expand host range) in 2 days, which matched the primary discovery of an independent, co-timed genomic+experimental study before that study completed peer review. The system itself did not run bacterial wet-lab experiments; the experimental confirmation was produced by the external study (refs 2 = Penades et al. Cell 2025; 21 = He et al. Cell 2025).
reverse_findings.loc: p.495 lines 1577-1588; Fig.1c bottom panel lines 311-334; abstract lines 37-40
reverse_findings.mismatch: None with claim C062 as written. C062 says 'with experimental biomedical validation' (unqualified), which the paper supports via the AML and liver-fibrosis in vitro experiments. The only over-generalization is internal to the paper: Fig.1c caption states 'All hypotheses generated by Co-Scientist were subsequently verified through independent in vitro laboratory experiments' (lines 413-414), whereas the AMR verification is an external co-timed study, not experiments driven by the system. This does not affect the manuscript claim.
